segments.text: Then Agrippa said to Paul,
You are permitted to speak for yourself.
segments.speaker: Agrippa

segments.text: So Paul stretched out his hand and answered for himself:
I think myself happy, King Agrippa, because today I shall answer for myself before you concerning all the things of which I am accused by the Jews,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: especially because you are expert in all customs and questions which have to do with the Jews. Therefore I beg you to hear me patiently.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: My manner of life from my youth, which was spent from the beginning among my own nation at Jerusalem, all the Jews know.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: They knew me from the first, if they were willing to testify, that according to the strictest sect of our religion I lived a Pharisee.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And now I stand and am judged for the hope of the promise made by God to our fathers.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: To this promise our twelve tribes, earnestly serving God night and day, hope to attain. For this hope's sake, King Agrippa, I am accused by the Jews.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Why should it be thought incredible by you that God raises the dead?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Indeed, I myself thought I must do many things contrary to the name of Jesus of Nazareth.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: This I also did in Jerusalem, and many of the saints I shut up in prison, having received authority from the chief priests; and when they were put to death, I cast my vote against them.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And I punished them often in every synagogue and compelled them to blaspheme; and being exceedingly enraged against them, I persecuted them even to foreign cities.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: While thus occupied, as I journeyed to Damascus with authority and commission from the chief priests,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: at midday, O king, along the road I saw a light from heaven, brighter than the sun, shining around me and those who journeyed with me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And when we all had fallen to the ground, I heard a voice speaking to me and saying in the Hebrew language,
Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting Me? It is hard for you to kick against the goads.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So I said, 'Who are You, Lord?'
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But rise and stand on your feet; for I have appeared to you for this purpose, to make you a minister and a witness both of the things which you have seen and of the things which I will yet reveal to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I will deliver you from the Jewish people, as well as from the Gentiles, to whom I now send you,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: to open their eyes, in order to turn them from darkness to light, and from the power of Satan to God, that they may receive forgiveness of sins and an inheritance among those who are sanctified by faith in Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore, King Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the heavenly vision,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: but declared first to those in Damascus and in Jerusalem, and throughout all the region of Judea, and then to the Gentiles, that they should repent, turn to God, and do works befitting repentance.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For these reasons the Jews seized me in the temple and tried to kill me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore, having obtained help from God, to this day I stand, witnessing both to small and great, saying no other things than those which the prophets and Moses said would come—
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: that the Christ would suffer, that He would be the first to rise from the dead, and would proclaim light to the Jewish people and to the Gentiles.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Now as he thus made his defense, Festus said with a loud voice,
Paul, you are beside yourself! Much learning is driving you mad!
segments.speaker: Festus

segments.text: I am not mad, most noble Festus, but speak the words of truth and reason.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For the king, before whom I also speak freely, knows these things; for I am convinced that none of these things escapes his attention, since this thing was not done in a corner.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know that you do believe.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: You almost persuade me to become a Christian.
segments.speaker: Agrippa

segments.text: I would to God that not only you, but also all who hear me today, might become both almost and altogether such as I am, except for these chains.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: When he had said these things, the king stood up, as well as the governor and Bernice and those who sat with them;
and when they had gone aside, they talked among themselves, saying,
This man is doing nothing deserving of death or chains.
segments.speaker: Unnamed Listeners

segments.text: This man might have been set free if he had not appealed to Caesar.
segments.speaker: Agrippa
